humanSummary.text: Re-audit found no remaining P0/P1 blockers after bidirectional proposal-link repair. Two non-blocking hardening findings from the audit pass are recorded as future DAG work.

findings.title: Proposal id format validation is too loose
findings.description: Style-guide proposal identifiers and turn proposal references are validated as non-blank strings rather than UUID7 identifiers.

evidence.detail: Initial audit found proposal.proposalId and turns[].proposalIds[] use generic string validation despite fixture conventions using UUID-shaped ids.

findings.impact: Malformed proposal identifiers can enter transcript fixtures and diagnostics, weakening later DB/repository linkage and machine routing.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: proposal.proposalId and turns[].proposalIds[] must validate as UUID7 identifiers or explicitly documented transitional ids.
Malformed proposal id diagnostics identify the exact turn id, field, proposal id, and validator rule.
Accepted, rejected, malformed, and conflicting transcript fixtures remain deterministic after id validation is tightened.

proposedDagNode.title: Style-guide proposal UUID7 validation
proposedDagNode.summary: Tighten style-guide conversation proposal id validation so proposal references use UUID7-shaped identifiers with precise diagnostics.
deliverables: Diagnostic assertions for proposal id fields
acceptanceCriteria: proposal.proposalId and turns[].proposalIds[] reject non-UUID7 identifiers unless explicitly documented as transitional fixture ids.
Diagnostics name the turn id, field, proposal id, and validator rule for malformed proposal identifiers.
Existing style-guide conversation fixtures continue to validate or are migrated to the documented id contract.

findings.title: Raw-private-field privacy regressions need explicit tests
findings.description: The recursive private-field/path guard exists, but tests emphasize unredacted examples rather than direct raw prompt, response, raw text, and private-local path keys.

evidence.detail: Audit found privacy validation implementation, but no explicit regression mutations for promptText, completionText, rawText, responseBody, or fixtures/private-local references.

findings.impact: Future refactors could weaken the raw private payload guard without a focused regression catching it.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Style-guide conversation tests reject promptText, completionText, rawText, responseBody, and fixtures/private-local references.
Privacy diagnostics name the turn id, field path, and privacy validator rule.
Valid redacted public fixtures continue to pass.

proposedDagNode.title: Style-guide raw-private-field privacy regressions
proposedDagNode.summary: Add explicit privacy regression coverage for raw provider text, raw response bodies, and private-local paths in style-guide conversation fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: Transcripts containing promptText, completionText, rawText, responseBody, or fixtures/private-local references fail validation.
Privacy diagnostics include turn id, exact field path, and privacy rule.
Accepted redacted public style-guide transcript fixtures remain valid.
